title: grants — regulatory compliance
description: Compliance controls this module produces evidence for. Grants are the primary access-provisioning mechanism outside RBAC — every issue, patch, revoke is audit-worthy. Deny grants are inviolable blocks that are SOC 2 CC6.3 evidence for tenant-authorised access controls.

controls.evidence: policies.json + permissions.json — every grant mutation gated by a named ability + guard. Cross-tenant + cross-guard writes refused at the observer + policy layers with dedicated error codes (GRANT_CROSS_TENANT, GRANT_PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION).

Every grant mutation writes (a) an audit row via HasAudit (per-column diff, 7-year retention), (b) an activity_log entry via HasActivityLog, (c) a broadcast event on tenant.{id}.grants. The `reason` column is REQUIRED, min 20 chars, retained indefinitely — every access provisioning decision has a human justification worth reading.
Grant read + write endpoints require TLS 1.2+ (Sanctum PATs + platform admin JWTs). Every mutation is signed by the auth_user PAT and cross-referenced against IP + user_agent in the activity log.
health.json + metrics.json + grants:audit-report weekly command output. High-signal metrics (self_service_grant_attempts, cross_tenant_attempts, denials_issued) alert on anomalies.
feature-flags.json — every material behaviour change ships behind a rollout flag with the 90-day retirement policy. `grants:review-flags` monthly report tracks flag ages.
GrantOverlayResolver enforces the D-A3 overlay chain (deny wins, allow overlays RBAC, RBAC roles, delegation). Every check emits a metric so hit rates + latency are observable.
grants:issue path (manual + access_request + delegation + bulk_provision) all thread through GrantIssuer::issue() which enforces the six invariants uniformly. No back-channel writes.

grants:audit-principal + grants:audit-resource commands + the features.json:grants.access_reviews quarterly workflow. Compliance officers can walk every user's grants + every resource's grantees against organisational policy.

grants:revoke path via GrantRevoker::revoke() sets revoked_at + revoked_by + revoked_reason atomically. Deny grants can never be silently deleted — they anonymise on erasure. Tenant erasure cascades all grants via FK.

Grant creation is a directly-solicited access change by the grantor (tenant admin OR user with grants.issue). Subject notification (GrantIssuedNotification) fires so the subject knows about their new access.
Grants store only the (subject, resource, permissions, reason) tuple. No profile data, no communication content. reason is optional-to-content but required-to-existence.
retention.json — allow grants retained 90 days post-terminal; deny grants retained indefinitely per audit_log tier; reason column retained indefinitely.
PurgeGrantsForErasedIdentity listener on user::IdentityErased. Allow grants hard-deleted; deny grants anonymised (subject_id → hash) but preserved for audit.

The six-invariant guard set (cross-tenant refusal, privilege-escalation, permission-catalogue check, past-dated refusal, immutability after issue, revoke-only-through-revoker) is enforced at BOTH the observer AND the issuer/action layer — defence in depth.
Encrypted at rest via Postgres TDE. Access logged via HasAudit + HasActivityLog. Rate-limited endpoints prevent enumeration.

GrantCrossTenantAttempt event routes to platform ops via TenantIsolationBreachNotification — tenant-isolation breach is a reportable incident. SelfServiceGrantAttempted alerts fire on repeated attempts (compromise indicator).

hipaa.note: Grants substrate itself is not HIPAA-touching, but grants ON healthcare-adjacent resources (athlete medical clearances, athlete injuries) inherit the resource's HIPAA classification. Downstream consumer modules (e.g. a future medical-records module) declare the HIPAA classification on their grantable resources.

controls.evidence: Same as GDPR Art. 17. PurgeGrantsForErasedIdentity listener handles cascade.

The share-access dialog surfaces every guard failure inline (subject can't grant permissions they lack, cross-tenant refused with explanation) before submit. The `grants/preflight` endpoint powers this.
Grant notification emails carry the resource name + grantor name in the CTA so 'View your new access' is unambiguous out of context.
